pages.full_text: This is a picture of a old chapell in [?Caunor] campsite it [is] now a barn wich is [braking] to [?buses]
pages.notes: Page contains a child's pencil drawing of a chapel/barn-like building with a triangular roof, oval window, and rectangular door, with block-like structures around the base. The handwritten text appears inside/around the drawing. The campsite name is uncertain — could read 'Caunor', 'Caunton', or another name. The final word is unclear — possibly 'pieces' or 'buses'. The letter 'A' appears to be a label within the drawing, not a page number. No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner.

pages.full_text:
When we arrived at __Chatsworth__ we went to look at the Cascades. We balanced lunch in the carpark on the side of a hill
    Simon took John, Charles & Pete to
pages.notes: Text is cut off at the bottom of the page — the sentence 'Simon took John, Charles & Pete to' continues on the next page. A colour postcard/photograph of Chatsworth House and its grounds is glued into the lower half of the page. No handwritten page number visible in top-right corner. The context (Chatsworth, Peak District) is consistent with the diary entry on earlier spread pages referencing a Peak District trip.
